task.title: Generate API Documentation
task.shortdesc: Generate and preview the API documentation that Postman builds automatically from your collection descriptions, requests, and saved examples.

prereq.p: Before you generate documentation, ensure that you have written descriptions for your collection, folders, and requests. For instructions, see .

context.p: Postman automatically assembles your collection descriptions, request details, parameter tables, and saved examples into a formatted documentation page. You can preview this page before publishing it.

step.cmd: In the sidebar, hover over the collection name and select the ... (More Actions) icon.

step.stepresult: Postman opens the documentation preview panel on the right side of the workbench. The preview shows the rendered documentation as it will appear when published.

step.cmd: Review the documentation preview to verify that all descriptions, parameters, headers, and example responses are accurate and complete.

info.p: Check the following elements in the preview:

ul.li: Collection title and description appear at the top of the page.
Folder names appear as section headings.
Each request shows the HTTP method, URL, description, parameters, and headers.
Saved example responses appear in a code block under each request.

step.cmd: If the documentation requires changes, close the preview panel, update the relevant descriptions or requests, and reopen the documentation view.

To select an environment for the documentation, open the environment selector in the upper-right corner of Postman and select the environment whose variable values you want Postman to use in the documentation examples.

info.p: Postman substitutes environment variable values into the URLs and parameters shown in the documentation preview. Select the environment that reflects the public-facing API endpoint.

r.p: Postman generates and displays the documentation preview based on your collection content. The documentation is ready to publish when the preview accurately reflects your API.

postreq.p: Publish the documentation to make it available to API consumers. For instructions, see .
